LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HARRY WOO'S MOTION TO STRIKE S.L.A.P.P SUIT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. ALTHOUGH CERTAIN PARAGRAPHS OF THE COMPLAINT REFER TO PROTECTED ACTIVITY, THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE PREMISED PREDOMINANTLY ON ACTIVITY NOT SUBJECT TO CCP 425.16. PARAGRAPH 33 IS ILLUSTRATIVE AND REFLECTS AN ALLEGED VIOLATION OF PLAINTIFFS' RIGHTS FOR VIOLATION OF STATUTES, ORDINANCES, LAWS, CODES AND REGULATIONS. UNLAWFUL RENT INCREASES, INVASION OF PRIVACY, NUISANCE, BREACH OF AN ORAL AGREEMENT AND THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING ARE NOT PROTECTED ACTIVITY. THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOCUSES PRIMARILY ON THE PROTECTED ACTIVITY OF FILING SUIT AGAINST PLAINTIFFS AND THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION SEEKS, AMONG OTHER THINGS, TO ENJOIN DEFENDANT FROM PROSECUTING A PENDING ACTION. PLAINTIFFS HAVE PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT WOULD BE SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR PREVAILING ON THEIR CLAIM THAT UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION WAS FILED IN BAD FAITH AND IN RETALIATION FOR PLAINTIFF'S EXERCISE OF THEIR RIGHT TO SEEK RELIEF FROM THE RENT BOARD. AS TO THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS SEEK INJUNCTIVE RELIEF RELATED TO MATTERS IN ADDITION TO THE OTHER PENDING ACTION AND SINCE THERE IS A BASIS TO CONCLUDE THE OTHER CASE WAS NOT FILED IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFFS HAVE DEMONSTRATED A BASIS FOR PREVAILING IF IT IS ASSUMED DEFENDANT HAS MET HIS BURDEN. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CARL SAUCEDA, CSR 2031

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS RONALD DONG, JEANETTE CORDERO, ANGELA POLAND AND DESIREE POLAND'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - OVERRULED; THE MOVING PARTIES ARE TO ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS. THE DECISION IN SMITH V. EXXON MOBIL OIL CORP. (2007) 153 CAL. APP. 4TH 1407, IS THE BASIS FOR THE COURT'S DECISION. THE RECORD DOES NOT SUPPORT THE CONCLUSION THAT WOO HAD A FULL AND FAIL OPPORTUNITY TO LITIGATE THE ISSUES PRESENTED BY THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. HE DID NOT APPEAR BECAUSE OF A MISUNDERSTANDING ON THE PART OF HIS COUNSEL; DISCOVERY WAS NOT AVAILABLE IN THE PRIOR PROCEEDING; WOO HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A QUESTION AS TO DONG'S STATUS; AND THE CROSS-COMPLAINT INCLUDES PARTIES WHO WERE INVOLVED IN THE RENT BOARD PROCEEDING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Oct-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS RONALD DONG, JEANETTE CORDERO, ANGELA POLAND AND DESIREE POLAND'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - overruled; the moving parties are to answer within 10 days. The decision in Smith v. Exxon Mobil Oil Corp. (2007) 153 Cal. App. 4th 1407, is the basis for the Court's decision. The record does not support the conclusion that Woo had a full and fail opportunity to litigate the issues presented by the cross-complaint. He did not appear because of a misunderstanding on the part of his counsel; discovery was not available in the prior proceeding; Woo has presented evidence that raises a question as to Dong's status; and the cross-complaint includes parties who were involved in the Rent Board proceeding. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion To Strike S.L.A.P.P Suit
Off Calendar Jun-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HARRY WOO'S MOTION TO STRIKE S.L.A.P.P SUIT.
APPEARANCES: WELDON BRADSTREET, ESQUIRE (SHEPPARD ROSEN LAW FIRM, 415.296.0900, X26) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. KAREN Y. UCHIYAMA, ESQUIRE (415.53.9301) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: denied. Although certain paragraphs of the complaint refer to protected activity, the causes of action are premised predominantly on activity not subject to CCP 425.16. Paragraph 33 is illustrative and reflects an alleged violation of plaintiffs' rights for violation of statutes, ordinances, laws, codes and regulations. Unlawful rent increases, invasion of privacy, nuisance, breach of an oral agreement and the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing are not protected activity. The fifth cause of action focuses primarily on the protected activity of filing suit against plaintiffs and the 11th cause of action seeks, among other things, to enjoin defendant from prosecuting a pending action. Plaintiffs have presented evidence that would be sufficient to establish a basis for prevailing on their claim that unlawful detainer action was filed in bad faith and in retaliation for plaintiff's exercise of their right to seek relief from the Rent Board. As to the 11th cause of action, plaintiffs seek injunctive relief related to matters in addition to the other pending action and since there is a basis to conclude the other case was not filed in good faith, plaintiffs have demonstrated a basis for prevailing if it is assumed defendant has met his burden. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CARL SAUCEDA, CSR 2031
